jokes.text: Mirror, mirror on the wall I ain't too short, I ain't too tall / Damn, if I don't have it all
jokes.setup: Will looking in mirror doing a confident self-assessment rap
jokes.punchline: The cocky rhyme and his obvious pride in his appearance
jokes.explanation: Classic Will Smith swagger and vanity, well-integrated character moment with decent wordplay

jokes.text: It just has way too many buttons and the color is very '95
jokes.setup: Hilary critiquing Will's fashion choice
jokes.punchline: Saying the color is 'very '95' - implying it's outdated
jokes.explanation: Good fashion snark from Hilary, topical for the era and character-appropriate

jokes.text: Girl, you must be crazy. I look great. I'm the man when it comes to fashion
jokes.setup: Will defending his fashion sense after criticism
jokes.punchline: His absolute confidence despite being told he looks bad
jokes.explanation: Standard Will arrogance joke, works but not exceptional

jokes.text: I'm gonna go ahead and change
jokes.setup: Will's confident fashion defense, then someone enters
jokes.punchline: Will immediately caves and decides to change clothes
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic reversal - his confidence completely crumbles with someone's mere presence

jokes.text: Oh, please, that woman turned more tricks than David Copperfield
jokes.setup: Discussion about whether a prostitute defendant is innocent or guilty
jokes.punchline: Playing on 'tricks' meaning both magic tricks and prostitution
jokes.explanation: Clever double entendre with perfect setup, very quotable line

jokes.text: Seems to me, community service is what got her in this mess in the first place. Serving the community
jokes.setup: Someone suggests she'll get community service
jokes.punchline: Carlton's interpretation that prostitution IS serving the community
jokes.explanation: Excellent Carlton moment - his earnestness making an innocent observation that's actually risqué

jokes.text: Boy, are there gonna be a lot of lonely guys this Friday night
jokes.setup: Guilty verdict announced for prostitute
jokes.punchline: Will's observation about the practical impact on her clients
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor with slight edge

jokes.text: Well, you deserved it. I mean, getting everybody's hopes up like that and then showing up again
jokes.setup: Carlton saying he was grounded for coming home late
jokes.punchline: Will implying Carlton should have stayed away permanently
jokes.explanation: Brutal but hilarious burn on Carlton, perfectly captures Will's attitude toward his cousin

jokes.text: Oh, thank God. I thought it was for me
jokes.setup: Death threat against Uncle Phil is announced
jokes.punchline: Carlton's relief that it's not for him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton self-centeredness - more worried about himself than his father

jokes.text: There's a dude here from Appalachia. He says he thinks your mouth is kind of pretty
jokes.setup: Will describing fan mail Uncle Phil is getting
jokes.punchline: The oddly specific and inappropriate compliment from a male fan
jokes.explanation: Funny absurd detail that feels authentic to fan mail weirdness

jokes.text: Guess I'll put this in the hug pile
jokes.setup: The previous inappropriate fan mail
jokes.punchline: Will's matter-of-fact categorization of the creepy letter
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan follow-up to the previous joke

jokes.text: He's the nut we working with. Heh-heh-heh
jokes.setup: Police officer asks who Carlton is after his security speech
jokes.punchline: Will identifying Carlton as 'the nut' they're working with
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/payoff with Carlton's over-the-top security planning

jokes.text: I don't usually say this, but I agree with the little squidget
jokes.setup: Will agreeing with Carlton about Uncle Phil needing protection
jokes.punchline: Calling Carlton 'the little squidget' while agreeing with him
jokes.explanation: 'Squidget' is a great made-up insult that sounds perfectly like Will

jokes.text: No, his family moved out of town. I ain't seen him since third grade
jokes.setup: Will tells dramatic story of Bobo Anderson who 'disappeared' after threats
jokes.punchline: Anti-climactic reveal that he just moved away as a kid
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection joke - builds dramatic tension then deflates it perfectly

jokes.text: Damn, I miss Bobo
jokes.setup: The previous Bobo Anderson story
jokes.punchline: Will's genuine nostalgia for his childhood friend
jokes.explanation: Sweet callback that humanizes the previous joke

jokes.text: Hmm. And you're a pint-size simulated male
jokes.setup: Carlton reading about Safety Guy being a 'life-size simulated male'
jokes.punchline: Will pointing out Carlton is a 'pint-size simulated male'
jokes.explanation: Great wordplay turning the product description into a Carlton insult

jokes.text: Huh. He must be on a break, huh?
jokes.setup: Will testing if Safety Guy can protect someone by presumably attacking it
jokes.punchline: Safety Guy failing to defend itself, Will's deadpan observation
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy with deadpan delivery

jokes.text: Yo, I can see right through your clothes with these things. No wonder you're so shy
jokes.setup: Will trying on infrared goggles
jokes.punchline: Claiming to see through Carlton's clothes and insulting what he sees
jokes.explanation: Standard Will insult but well-executed with the gadget setup

jokes.text: Laugh
jokes.setup: Carlton asking what Will should do if they get caught
jokes.punchline: Will's simple, honest answer about his likely reaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - one word that perfectly captures Will's attitude toward Carlton's plans

jokes.text: Who needs brains when you got a body like that?
jokes.setup: Will telling Hilary that Safety Guy is a dummy
jokes.punchline: Hilary's shallow response about the mannequin's appearance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary moment - her vanity and superficiality on full display

jokes.text: Looks like your well-oiled machine just ran out of gas
jokes.setup: Carlton describing their operation as a 'well-oiled machine' followed by apparent failure
jokes.punchline: Uncle Phil's sarcastic callback to Carlton's metaphor
jokes.explanation: Good callback with Uncle Phil's dry wit deflating Carlton's pretensions

jokes.text: Just let him bang down a couple Ding Dongs. He'll be all right
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil being tense about the trial
jokes.punchline: Will's solution being Uncle Phil's apparent snack cake habit
jokes.explanation: Good character-based humor about Uncle Phil's eating habits

jokes.text: Hey, yo. Hey, man, where does that leave me? You are so selfish
jokes.setup: Carlton's escape plan that leaves Will behind with the bomber
jokes.punchline: Will calling Carlton selfish for not wanting to be left with a bomb
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's self-preservation and relationship with Carlton

jokes.text: Um... Wouldn't it be easier to just get a agent?
jokes.setup: Juggles explaining his bomb plan to get on TV
jokes.punchline: Will's logical, understated alternative suggestion
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response to an insane situation - highlights the absurdity through understatement

jokes.text: I had an agent and then he had my wife. Heh. That's a joke!
jokes.setup: Will's suggestion to get an agent
jokes.punchline: Juggles' bitter personal story followed by desperate clarification that it was meant to be funny
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Juggles' desperation and failed attempts at humor

jokes.text: I could've sold twice as many hamburgers as that Ronald guy. That no-act clown
jokes.setup: Juggles complaining about lack of opportunities
jokes.punchline: A clown calling Ronald McDonald a 'no-act clown'
jokes.explanation: Funny professional jealousy and the irony of one clown dissing another

jokes.text: One little tent fire. One little elephant stampede. A couple of kids end up in the hospital
jokes.setup: Juggles explaining why he can't get work
jokes.punchline: The increasingly serious nature of his 'little' incidents
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation comedy - each incident gets progressively worse while being downplayed

jokes.text: Thank you. Thank you very much. But I'm coming with you
jokes.setup: Juggles seeming grateful for the advice about the courthouse
jokes.punchline: The reveal that he's taking them hostage to go there
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that escalates the situation

jokes.text: What are we, Driving Miss Daisy?
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil driving slowly and carefully
jokes.punchline: Juggles' pop culture reference to the slow-driving movie
jokes.explanation: Decent pop culture reference but fairly obvious

jokes.text: Pedal to the metal, Tattoo
jokes.setup: Juggles wanting Uncle Phil to drive faster
jokes.punchline: Calling Carlton 'Tattoo' (reference to Fantasy Island character)
jokes.explanation: Good height joke with pop culture reference

jokes.text: Oh, the sight of that perfectly chiseled foam body being mutilated by that 18-wheeler. You bastard
jokes.setup: Reference to Juggles throwing Safety Guy out the window
jokes.punchline: Carlton's over-the-top emotional attachment to the dummy
jokes.explanation: Excellent Carlton moment - his dramatic overreaction to the dummy's destruction is perfectly in character

jokes.text: And you know how the ladies feel about clowns, huh? Huh? Ha-ha. Oh. You look like you might've had a little freaky rendezvous with the bearded lady
jokes.setup: Will trying to appeal to Juggles about women at the courthouse
jokes.punchline: Will's assumption about clowns' romantic success and specific circus reference
jokes.explanation: Will trying to relate to the clown through his own perspective on attracting women

jokes.text: But I work alone!
jokes.setup: Will suggesting they could work together
jokes.punchline: Juggles' dramatic rejection delivered like a movie villain
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Juggles' delusional self-importance

jokes.text: This is better than Benny Hill
jokes.setup: News coverage of the chaotic courtroom scene
jokes.punchline: Comparing the real-life chaos to slapstick comedy TV
jokes.explanation: Decent meta-joke about the absurdity of the situation

jokes.text: We all remember when Judge Lance Peterson cracked and rendered a verdict with the aide of a sock puppet named Randy
jokes.setup: News report about judges cracking under pressure
jokes.punchline: Specific absurd example with the sock puppet
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd detail that makes the current situation seem almost normal by comparison

jokes.text: Ernie!
jokes.setup: Magic trick goes wrong revealing a dead rabbit
jokes.punchline: Will's horrified reaction to the dead rabbit named Ernie
jokes.explanation: Good dark comedy moment with Will's genuine shock

jokes.text: You suck. All right? The reason you can't get work... you know, it's not because people don't like you, man... It's because you suck. You know? You're not funny. You suck
jokes.setup: Juggles asking why the crowd isn't laughing
jokes.punchline: Will's brutally honest assessment delivered with repetitive emphasis
jokes.explanation: Brutally honest Will moment - his blunt delivery makes it funnier than it should be

jokes.text: Carlton, Ernie is dead
jokes.setup: Carlton saying the crowd is dead, not Juggles
jokes.punchline: Will's literal correction about the actual dead rabbit
jokes.explanation: Good callback with Will's characteristic literalness cutting through Carlton's metaphor

jokes.text: Will was right. You do suck
jokes.setup: Juggles' failed grand finale
jokes.punchline: Uncle Phil agreeing with Will's earlier harsh assessment
jokes.explanation: Good callback with Uncle Phil's rare moment of supporting Will's rudeness

jokes.text: Oh, please. That ain't even right. I mean, he ain't insane. You know, he's just a clown that likes to make people laugh
jokes.setup: News report calling Juggles 'dangerously insane'
jokes.punchline: Will defending the bomb-wielding clown as just wanting to make people laugh
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's ability to empathize even with clearly problematic people

jokes.text: Hey, it's the dummy. Oh, yeah, and he found Safety Guy
jokes.setup: Ashley entering with Safety Guy
jokes.punchline: Will calling Ashley the dummy, then noting Safety Guy
jokes.explanation: Standard Will insult with callback to the mannequin subplot

jokes.text: You scream like a girl?
jokes.setup: Carlton saying they learned something about facing danger
jokes.punchline: Will's assumption about what Carlton learned
jokes.explanation: Classic Will interruption deflating Carlton's attempt at a serious moment

jokes.text: Well, buddy, looks like the security of the Banks family rests on our shoulders
jokes.setup: Carlton addressing Safety Guy after everyone else gets assignments
jokes.punchline: Carlton treating the mannequin as his partner
jokes.explanation: Nice button on the Safety Guy subplot with Carlton's continuing delusion
